title: Fatigable Weakness - Myasthenia Gravis
chief_complaint: Drooping eyelids and double vision for 2 months, worse in the evening. Difficulty chewing and swallowing late in the day.

physical_exam.neuro: Bilateral ptosis worsening with sustained upward gaze, diplopia on lateral gaze, fatiguable weakness of proximal muscles, normal reflexes, no sensory deficits

hidden_data.ct_chest: Anterior mediastinal mass consistent with thymoma

patient_responses.ptosis: My eyelids droop, especially when I'm tired
patient_responses.diplopia: I see double when I read or watch TV
patient_responses.chewing: I can't finish a meal, my jaw gets tired

feedback.correct_message: Excellent diagnosis! MG with thymoma - important to identify.
feedback.incorrect_message: This is myasthenia gravis. The fatiguable weakness and positive antibodies confirm it.
feedback.attending_note: MG management: Pyridostigmine for symptoms, immunosuppression (prednisone + azathioprine), thymectomy for thymoma. Avoid MG-exacerbating drugs. Monitor respiratory function for myasthenic crisis.
